payload.text:
Je veux que tu me fasse une pock de cette app mobile avec cette spec : LES BESOINS
 

OnlyJam est une application musicale sociale qui regroupe les fonctionnalités des plateformes de streaming et des réseaux sociaux afin de créer des sessions d’écoute collaboratives en temps réel.

L’objectif est de permettre à plusieurs personnes d’écouter et de construire une playlist commune, peu importe leur téléphone, leur casque ou leur plateforme musicale.

 

Comptes & accès
L’application est disponible sur iOS et Android afin d’être totalement multiplateforme.

Un nouvel utilisateur peut :

créer un compte avec photo, bio et profil identifiable,
ou se connecter rapidement via Google ou d’autres services.
L’onboarding est pensé pour être simple et accessible afin de rejoindre rapidement une session musicale.

 

Fonctionnement des Jams
Un utilisateur peut créer une session appelée Jam.

L’hôte :

génère un QR Code,
le partage aux participants,
garde le contrôle total de la playlist et des droits.
Les invités peuvent rejoindre instantanément la session en scannant le QR code.

 

Ajout de musique
Pendant la session :

les participants recherchent un morceau,
l’ajoutent à la Jam globale,
les musiques entrent dans une file d’attente commune.
Les invités peuvent :

voter pour les sons,
liker les musiques,
proposer des titres.
L’objectif est d’écouter une sélection variée construite par tout le groupe.

 

Profils & réseau social
OnlyJam intègre un aspect réseau social :

profils utilisateurs,
amis,
partage de playlists,
likes et interactions musicales.
Il est possible d’importer ses playlists depuis d’autres plateformes afin d’éviter de les recréer.

L’application conserve également l’historique des Jams passées.

 

Synchronisation & appareils
La musique peut être synchronisée sur plusieurs appareils :

plusieurs téléphones,
plusieurs casques,
ou via CarPlay pour diffuser dans la voiture.
Un téléphone peut aussi diffuser sur celui d’un autre utilisateur.

 

Rôles utilisateurs
Deux types de profils existent :

Hôte

contrôle administratif
accepte ou refuse les musiques
gère les droits
Invité

propose des sons
vote
interagit
Modèle économique
Le modèle économique s’inspire de la radio :

diffusion collective
découverte musicale
engagement communautaire

**Defining the MVP Scope**

Okay, I'm zeroing in on defining the MVP scope for "OnlyJam." It's a mobile web app built with React and Tailwind, focusing on real-time collaborative listening. I'm prioritizing user authentication as a core feature.


**Prioritizing Features Deeply**

I'm now focusing on breaking down essential features. I'm prioritizing user account creation and fast login (even if simulated initially). I've mapped out the session flow, from host creation to guest joining via QR codes. I am prioritizing music management functionality, including song search (simulated integrations), a shared queue, and a "Now Playing" screen. Social features are next on my list.


**Visualizing User Interface Designs**

I'm now iterating on the UI design. I've sketched out the crucial views: the host panel with QR codes and session controls, and the guest player, with search and "Now Playing" functionality, and advanced views showing notifications. I'm focusing on the user flow through these screens.


**Defining Technical Foundations**

I'm solidifying the technical foundations for this. I'll be leaning on `useState` and `Context` for state management, keeping it straightforward for this PoC. Integrating a library like `react-qr-code` will make the session joining easier. To bring it to life quickly, I'm hardcoding song data and initial queue state, which is a great starting point for now. Component structure is also in place, with core pages laid out.


**Implementing Core Navigation**

I'm now focusing on navigation. The app will feature a dark theme with red accents. The landing page has a prominent title and call to action. Host mode displays a QR code and session ID, with toggles for settings. Guest mode offers session ID input and queue viewing. The shared music player mocks "Now Playing" and includes play/pause/skip. Search incorporates a fake search bar and predefined results.

\n\n

**Prioritizing UI Enhancements**

I'm now refining the UI based on screenshots. A prominent "OnlyJam" logo sits in the top left, with a settings gear in the top right. The search bar will have a red outline upon focus, with filter pills.


**Refining UI Component Design**

I'm now iterating on the visual details. The music player card will use a dark design with a subtle gradient effect. The queue items will display album art, song details, the user who added the song, and a vote count. I'm focusing on the component-level designs now.

**Identifying Missing Imports**

I've pinpointed a common issue: "Cannot find name 'Settings'". This signals the `Settings` icon component, likely from `lucide-react`, is used but not imported. My current focus is to identify the exact line where the Settings component is referenced, which is line 13.


**Adding Missing Imports**

I've confirmed the `Settings` icon's absence in the import. It's used in `SearchView` component on line 231, inside the `div` element, as expected. The goal is to add the `Settings` import from `lucide-react` in `App.tsx` and now I know where it's used.


**Verifying File Content**

Now I have confirmed that the `Settings` component is used within the `App` component as well, specifically within the `ViewState.PROFILE` case. I've successfully added the `Settings` import to `App.tsx` from `lucide-react`, ensuring it's available for use. I am verifying the file contents to confirm the import and the component's presence.


**Analyzing Error Logs**

I'm now analyzing the error logs further. I was able to verify the errors pinpointed to `App.tsx` on lines 231 and 373, which both involve the `Settings` component. I will update `App.tsx`, and make a note of the locations. I also made a check for other errors, but the user only provided the two lines. The updates are in preparation to address the errors.


**Confirming Correct Imports**

I've reviewed the import statements in `App.tsx` and compared them against the provided code snippet. The import for `Settings` from `lucide-react` is correctly included, and no other imports are missing, so the import statements are correct. I'm satisfied all necessary dependencies are declared.


**Examining Missing Components**

I'm now investigating a potential missing import within the `<OnlyJam />` component. I've located the `<Play />` and `<Search />` components, which are used but could potentially be missing imports. I am going to verify those components have been declared.


**Locating UI Errors**

I'm now focusing on a potential error related to a missing closing tag in the UI, within the `HostPanel` component. The UI shows that something is missing, which is a closing tag. My current efforts are to confirm the location, and then fix the issue. I am reviewing the JSX structure, to identify the source and resolve the syntax error.


**Fixing UI Syntax**

I'm now zeroing in on a missing closing tag within the `HostPanel` component. The UI has a clear indication of this issue. I am reviewing the JSX structure carefully. After confirming the error location, I'll address the syntax error by ensuring the closing tag is present to make the UI function as expected.


**Confirming Component Properties**

I'm now investigating the properties of components, specifically the `ArrowRight` icon. I'm reviewing how the properties, such as `size` and `className`, are being applied to it. My goal is to verify that these properties are correctly passed to and used by the icon component, ensuring proper styling and behavior.


**Verifying New UI Elements**

I'm now focusing on a new UI element within a `div`. I'm going through the HTML tags. Specifically, I see a section for "Voting Enabled" and "Guest Uploads." I will make sure the elements have the correct visual styling. My aim is to make sure these elements appear and function as designed, ensuring they're properly integrated into the user interface.


**Inspecting UI Elements**

I'm now focusing on a new UI element, which seems to toggle "Allow new tracks." I've identified the use of `div` elements, a background color, and a circular toggle. I am reviewing the structure, and I see the need to check that this section renders correctly.


**Reviewing Component Structure**

I am now reviewing the structure of the UI components, focusing on the CD/Album Art section, and the related `div` elements, especially the live status and the like button. I need to make sure the structure and styling are accurate. I see the live status pill, the CD art, and the like button, and I will check their positioning and functionality.


**Checking Song Display**

I'm now investigating the song display section within the UI. I'm focusing on the heart icon, song title, artist information and progress bar. My priority is to confirm that these elements render correctly, including the heart icon, title, artist, and progress bar, and they all display the correct information.



**Analyzing UI Playback**

I'm now analyzing the UI playback controls, specifically the progress bar and playback buttons. I am making sure the progress bar updates correctly. I'm focusing on the shuffle, skip, and play/pause buttons, ensuring their styling and functionality are accurate, including hover effects. I am checking the current time display.


**Verifying Component Playback**

I'm now focused on the pause, skip, and repeat buttons in the UI. I have identified the correct icons: `Pause`, `SkipForward`, and `Repeat`. I will confirm their sizes and styling are correctly applied, ensuring the hover effects and colors are as intended. I'm also confirming these elements display and are functional, with all properties correctly applied.


**Examining Empty State**

I'm now investigating the "No songs in queue" message, checking when and how it displays. The conditional rendering using `session?.queue.length` is what I'm focused on. My plan is to make sure the message shows correctly when the queue is empty. I am checking the empty-state fallback to ensure it renders as expected.



**Confirming Search Functionality**

I'm now focusing on the search functionality within the UI. Specifically, I'm checking the `onChange` event on the input field to make sure that the `setQuery` state function is correctly managing the search input. My next task is to verify that the query term is passed.


**Validating Search Filtering**

I'm now diving into the filter functionality within the search component. I'm focusing on the `setFilter` state, along with the UI updates, like the background color changes. I need to make sure the filtering logic and UI updates work together correctly when selecting different filters, and that they update the component, especially the UI.



**Validating Results Display**

I'm now investigating the display of search results, specifically the "Top Results" and "Artists" sections. I see a `map` function iterating through an array, rendering `SongItem` components and artist elements. I need to verify data is presented correctly, the scrolling, and element spacing. I'll make sure each song displays correctly.


**Verifying Artist Rendering**

I'm now focusing on the rendering of artist thumbnails. I'm examining how each artist thumbnail is displayed, with the image source. I am focusing on making sure the artist images load, the fallback image if necessary, and the correct `alt` attributes. I'm confirming that the structure and display of the artist thumbnails is as intended.



**Confirming App Navigation**

I'm now investigating the app's navigation flow, particularly the transitions between `LandingPage`, `AUTH`, `HostPanel` and `Player` views. The `setView` state updates seem correct, and I'll confirm the rendering logic within `renderView` is managing these views and their respective components, specifically the `HostPanel`, and the `Player` view.


**Confirming Host-Panel Rendering**

I'm verifying the `HostPanel` is correctly rendered, specifically as an overlay on the `PlayerView`. The conditional rendering, triggered by `setShowHostPanel`, is what I'm focused on. My plan is to make sure it appears and disappears, and ensure its positioning is correct. I see its overlay, but need to make sure this is functional.



**Validating Profile Elements**

I'm now focusing on the profile section, analyzing user information and visual elements within the `div`. The avatar, user name, and role display properly, with host or music enthusiast, based on the data. I've verified the components, like the user name and avatar, display as intended.



**Reviewing Component Styling**

I'm now investigating the visual styling of several components in `ProfileView`. I've confirmed that the avatar, user name, and role are displaying, but I need to make sure the "Settings" icon and log-out button are styled as expected. I will focus on the classes within the `div` and `Button` components.

<!-- Add comment above each fix -->

payload.text:
Accessibilité et Onboarding (Inscription) (plan technique/fonctionnalités classiques)
Accès Multi-plateforme : En tant qu'utilisateur, être capable de télécharger l'application sur iOS et Android ou accéder à une Web App via une URL, afin de pouvoir participer quel que soit l’appareil.
Possibilité de création de compte : En tant que nouvel utilisateur, pouvoir créer un profil (avec une photo, bio, pseudo), afin d'être identifiable dans les Jams et sur les réseaux sociaux par exemple, avoir un suivi, etc.
Possibilité de connexion rapide : En tant qu'utilisateur, pouvoir s'inscrire/se connecter via Google/Apple/Facebook, afin de gagner du temps.
2. Création et Gestion de la Jam (Côté Hôte)
Création de Jam : En tant qu'Hôte, je veux lancer une session "Jam" rapidement, afin de commencer à diffuser de la musique.
Génération QR Code : En tant qu'Hôte, je veux générer un QR Code unique (et un lien de partage), afin que mes amis puissent rejoindre la session instantanément.
Gestion des Droits (Volume) : En tant qu'Hôte, je veux configurer si les invités peuvent ou non modifier le volume général, afin d'éviter les abus en soirée.
Mode "Suggestion" : En tant qu'Hôte, je veux activer un mode "Approbation requise", afin que les musiques ajoutées par les invités ne se lancent pas avant que je les aie validées.
Modération : En tant qu'Hôte, je veux pouvoir bannir un utilisateur de la Jam, afin de maintenir une bonne ambiance.
Priorité Hôte : En tant qu'Hôte, je veux pouvoir passer (skip) ou supprimer n'importe quelle musique de la file d'attente, même si ce n'est pas moi qui l'ai ajoutée.
3. Participation à la Jam (Côté Invité)
Rejoindre par Scan : En tant qu'Invité, je veux scanner un QR Code pour atterrir directement dans la Jam active sans navigation complexe.
Recherche Universelle (La fonctionnalité clé) : En tant qu'Invité, je veux rechercher un titre dans une barre de recherche unique qui agrège les résultats de toutes les plateformes (Spotify, YouTube, SoundCloud, Apple Music), afin de trouver n'importe quelle musique existante.
Upload Fichier Local : En tant qu'Invité, je veux pouvoir uploader un fichier audio (MP3/WAV) depuis mon téléphone vers la Jam, afin de passer une prod ou un son qui n'est pas sur les plateformes.
Ajout à la File : En tant qu'Invité, je veux ajouter un son à la file d'attente (queue), afin qu'il soit joué après les morceaux en cours.
4. Aspect Réseau Social & Profil
Import de Playlists : En tant qu'utilisateur, je veux connecter mes comptes (Spotify/Deezer) pour importer mes playlists personnelles sur mon profil, afin de les retrouver facilement lors d'une Jam.
Vitrine Musicale : En tant qu'utilisateur, je veux épingler mes 3 sons favoris du moment sur mon profil, afin de montrer mes goûts musicaux aux autres.
Système d'Amis : En tant qu'utilisateur, je veux envoyer et recevoir des demandes d'amis, afin de créer ma communauté.
Historique des Jams : En tant qu'utilisateur, je veux retrouver l'historique des morceaux joués lors d'une précédente Jam, afin de réécouter une découverte musicale.
5. Fonctionnalités Avancées & Techniques
Jam "Sync" (Co-organisée) : En tant que groupe d'amis, nous voulons activer le mode "Synchro", afin que la musique soit jouée exactement en même temps sur tous nos téléphones (ex: pour une Silent Disco ou amplifier le son avec plusieurs téléphones).
Support CarPlay / Android Auto : En tant que conducteur, je veux une interface simplifiée sur l'écran de ma voiture, afin de voir le titre en cours et passer les musiques en toute sécurité.
Gestion du Cache : En tant qu'Hôte, je veux que les 2 ou 3 prochaines musiques soient pré-chargées, afin d'éviter les coupures si le réseau devient instable.

**Initiating the PoC**

I'm starting the Proof of Concept for "OnlyJam." My initial setup includes HTML, TypeScript, and React components – specifically `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, `types.ts`, `constants.ts`, and a `jamService.ts` file. My focus is on getting the basic structure in place.


**Expanding the Component Library**

I've expanded the component library, incorporating `Button.tsx`, `SongItem.tsx`, `Header.tsx`, and `Navigation.tsx`. Plus, I've added a `metadata.json` file. Now, I'm shifting to a plan for incorporating the user stories. This is where the core functionality begins to take shape. I am starting to think about my plan for updates based on the user's requirements.

**Analyzing User Stories**

My focus is shifting to the user stories. I need to simulate mobile behavior on a web app. I'm breaking down the stories, like onboarding/auth, host features, and guest features. I'm thinking of creating the account with photo, bio, and pseudo, alongside quick login with Google, Apple, and Facebook simulations.


**Defining Data Structures**

I'm updating `User` to include a bio, pinned songs, and friends. I'm also updating `JamSession` with `approvalRequired`, `guestsCanControlVolume`, `connectedUsers`, and `history`. Plus, I'm adding types for `Playlist`. I'm starting to think about mock data for playlists, history, and friends to be added to `constants.ts`.


**Implementing New Features**

I'm adding friend lists, request views, and history. I've integrated a "Car Mode" toggle for a simplified view, and updated `jamService` to handle new queue logic, including an approval queue. The UI design will maintain dark mode with neon red branding and be mobile-first. I'm incorporating `lucide-react` icons. Specifically, I'm simulating auth via a simple form and faking a file upload.


**Adding New Features**

I am now focusing on implementing the new features of `VolumeControl` and `syncMode`. I have added mock playlists and history data to `constants.ts`. I've added a new `CarView.tsx` component, introducing a simplified driving interface. The `App.tsx` file is being updated to integrate a new state management approach for the approval queue, add Car View and enhance the Profile and Search views, along with updating the Host Panel. I am also implementing the authentication updates, and all other updates to host and guest components per the user story updates.


**Refining UI Components**

I'm focusing on UI components, adding "Pinned Songs", "Imported Playlists" (with Spotify/Deezer icons), a Friends count/button, and History tab to the Profile. "Car Mode" is getting big buttons (Play/Pause, Skip), large text, and a simplified interface accessible via settings. I will need to update `types.ts` and `constants.ts` to support the changes. The `App.tsx` file will undergo significant changes to wire everything up, with a new `components/CarView.tsx` file for "Car Mode."


**Expanding Data Structures**

I've updated `types.ts` to reflect a more robust `User` structure, now including `bio`, `pinnedSongs`, `friends`, and `playlists`. Similarly, `JamSession` has been updated with `approvalQueue`, `history`, `connectedUsers`, and new `settings`. I've started the implementation for the new `CarView.tsx` component, including UI changes in `App.tsx` by adding `CAR_MODE`. My focus is now on the Universal Search feature. I'm building tabs for various sources, including a local mock file upload, enhancing the user experience.


**Implementing Host Approval**

I'm focused on "Host Approval" and "Sync Mode". I'm updating `types.ts` for interfaces and `constants.ts` with mock data. I'm prioritizing the Proof of Concept, and I'll create `CarView.tsx` with a car icon in `Header.tsx`. I'll implement approval logic and user profiles in `App.tsx`, and universal search functionality. The focus will be on appearance and functionality.

To transform the current application into the robust Proof of Concept (PoC) described in the specification, I will make the following changes:

1.  **Update `types.ts`**: Expand the data models to support user profiles (bio, friends), Jam settings (approval mode, sync mode, volume control), and the new song sources (Local, SoundCloud, Apple Music).
2.  **Update `constants.ts`**: Add mock data for the new social features and extended song library.
3.  **Update `components/SongItem.tsx`**: Add support for approval/rejection actions (for the Host) and source icons for the new platforms.
4.  **Create `components/CarView.tsx`**: A new, simplified interface optimized for driving (CarPlay/Android Auto style).
5.  **Update `App.tsx`**:
    *   **Onboarding**: Add a profile creation step.
    *   **Profile**: Enhance with pinned songs, playlists, and friends.
    *   **Search**: Add source tabs (Spotify, YT, Local, etc.) and file upload simulation.
    *   **Host Panel**: Add controls for Sync Mode, Approval Mode, and Guest Volume.
    *   **Player**: Implement the "Approval Queue" and logic for moving songs from pending to active.

Here is the implementation:

entries.description: Update types for Profile, Social features, and Jam settings
Update mock data with new sources and profile info

diagnostics.message: Type '{ votingEnabled: true; guestUploads: false; explicitFilter: false; }' is missing the following properties from type '{ votingEnabled: boolean; guestUploads: boolean; explicitFilter: boolean; requireApproval: boolean; guestVolumeControl: boolean; syncMode: boolean; }': requireApproval, guestVolumeControl, syncMode

Type '{ size: number; className: string; title: string; }' is not assignable to type 'IntrinsicAttributes & Omit<LucideProps, "ref"> & RefAttributes<SVGSVGElement>'.
Property 'title' does not exist on type 'IntrinsicAttributes & Omit<LucideProps, "ref"> & RefAttributes<SVGSVGElement>'.

payload.text:
**Addressing TypeScript Errors**

I'm currently focused on addressing the TypeScript errors, starting with `services/jamService.ts`. I see the `JamSession['settings']` type definition has been updated in `types.ts`, which now includes `requireApproval`, `guestVolumeControl`, and `syncMode`. I'm going to update the code to comply with this updated type.


**Resolving Inconsistencies in Types**

I've identified that the `createJam` function in `jamService.ts` is missing the `requireApproval`, `guestVolumeControl`, and `syncMode` properties in its return value. I'm going to add these properties to ensure the returned object aligns with the updated `JamSession['settings']` type definition. This should resolve the inconsistency in the return type.


**Fixing React TypeScript Errors**

I'm tackling React TypeScript errors. I found that I need to add the `X` icon from `lucide-react` to the import list in `App.tsx` on line 89, because the `HostPanel` component uses it but it is not imported. Also on line 413, the `Cloud` icon is not imported. To fix it, I need to add it to the import list in `App.tsx` as well.


**Importing Required Icons**

I'm now focusing on importing the `Cloud` icon from `lucide-react` into `App.tsx`. The component uses the icon, but it's not currently imported, resulting in errors. I've pinpointed the exact line that needs correction and I'm updating the import list accordingly to resolve this. It's a straightforward fix to prevent runtime issues.


**Updating Jam Session Settings**

I'm now focusing on fixing a type mismatch error in the `settings` property of `JamSession`. The error message precisely highlights the missing properties: `requireApproval`, `guestVolumeControl`, and `syncMode`. I'm going to update the code, in particular `createJam`, to include these. I'll make sure all the properties in the `settings` property are present according to the new type definition.


**Adding Missing Properties**

Now, I'm extending the `createJam` function to include all missing properties, not just those related to the `settings` object. I noticed the function was also omitting `approvalQueue`, `history`, and `connectedUsers` at the top level of the `JamSession` object. entries.description: Add missing properties (approvalQueue, history, connectedUsers, and new settings) to satisfy JamSession interface.
Wrap Smartphone icon in a span to support title prop without type error.
Add missing imports for X and Cloud icons from lucide-react.
